content.problemStatement:
Little Petya likes integer numbers a lot. Recently he has received a sequence of integers as a gift from his mother. Petya calls a sequence of **N** integers **Ai** beautiful if there exist a sequence of **N** integer numbers **Bi** for which the following conditions hold:



 At least one number **Bi** differs from 0. 
 For every **j** from 0 to **N**-1 the following holds: 
 **A0* Bj** + **A1* B(j + 1) mod N** + ... + **AN-1* B(j + N - 1) mod N** = 0.

**Ai**
constraints: Sum of N for all test cases in a single input file will not exceed 150 000
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N on the first line, followed by N space-separated integers A0, A1, ..., AN-1 on the second line.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if Petya's sequence is beautiful and NO otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there is no sequence B that satisfies the conditions. For the second test case, one possible sequence B is (1, -1, 0). For the third test case, one possible sequence B is (1, 1, 1, 1). For the fourth test case, there is no sequence B that satisfies the conditions.
For the test case, one possible sequence B is (0).
For the test case, one possible sequence B is (1, -1).

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by constructing a matrix from the given sequence and checking its rank. If the rank of the matrix is less than N, then the sequence is beautiful. Otherwise, it is not. The matrix is constructed by shifting the sequence and taking the dot product with the original sequence.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^3) per test case, O(T * N^3) total. The dominant operation is the computation of the rank of the matrix.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — the dominant structure is the matrix.
